title: Contract Schema
intro: Contracts are JSON files that define API behavior in a deterministic format.
mentalModel: A contract is the API truth source. Your app code consumes generated artifacts from that truth instead of duplicating endpoint types manually across layers.
requiredItems: Each endpoint defines method, path and response statuses.
endpointItems: `response`: object keyed by HTTP status code.

fieldGuide.name: service
fieldGuide.meaning: Logical namespace for endpoints inside a single contract file.

fieldGuide.name: path + method
fieldGuide.meaning: Unique operation identity. Together they define routing and generated method signatures.

fieldGuide.name: params / query / body
fieldGuide.meaning: Typed input channels. Generated clients and validators use these shapes directly.

fieldGuide.name: response
fieldGuide.meaning: Map of status code -> payload shape. Drives output typings and compatibility checks.
fieldGuide.example: Example: `200`, `201`, `400`, `404` each with explicit payload schema.

notes: Keep field names stable to avoid accidental breaking changes.
Prefer explicit response shapes for all returned status codes.
Use `ckit:validate` as a fast schema guard.
commonMistakes: Changing field names in `response` payloads without running `check`.
Using inconsistent status mappings between endpoints for similar outcomes.
Leaving optional input fields undocumented and relying on app-side assumptions only.
Treating generated artifacts as editable source files instead of generated output.
